title: STAT+: Moderna to pay Roivant up to $2.25 billion to settle patent lawsuit behind mRNA vaccines
author: Jason Mast
fullText:
‘This was a case that should have settled at the very beginning’

 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Ruby Wallau for STAT

 
 Jason Mast is a general assignment reporter at STAT focused on the science behind new medicines and the systems and people that decide whether that science ever reaches patients. You can reach Jason on Signal at JasonMast.05.

 Moderna has agreed to pay Roivant up to $2.25 billion to settle claims that the mRNA vaccine developer infringed on Roivant’s patents in its Covid-19 shot.

Roivant will receive $950 million and then another $1.3 billion if Moderna’s attempts to have parts of its liability offloaded to the federal government fail upon appeal. If the full amount is paid, it will be among the largest patent settlements in history. 

“It is probably the largest ever,” said Jacob Sherkow, a professor of law and medicine at the University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign.

summary: A settlement provides a massive windfall for Arbutus and Roivant and avoids a worst-case scenario for Moderna.
